description: A small bowl-shaped container for drinking; a vessel lifted to lips. 'Soeli' names what brings drink to mouth—the intimate vessel of refreshment.
sound_symbolism: The word sips gently. 'So' begins with soft approach, vessel lifted. The vowel 'e' opens to receive. Then 'li' flows smoothly, drink entering.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'mia soeli phelu.' (I hold the cup.) For tea, water, wine—any drinking vessel held in hand. Compare 'noru' (bowl) and 'molu' (container).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Drinking mindfully—the cup invites presence, each sip an act of awareness.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The cup as ritual—tea ceremony, shared wine, the vessel that brings drink to community.
